title: I/O Contracts, Files, Serialization, Time, and Command-Line Programs · Language Lens: Reader Writer Streaming
summary: A command must stream records, validate JSON, write atomically, handle time and cancellation, and produce useful exit evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go reader writer streaming increment in a deterministic import and reporting command. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose io.Reader and io.Writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer.
Run representative workloads with the race detector, interpret reports, retain reproductions, and state unexecuted-path limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Reader Writer Streaming · Theory Reader Writer Streaming
steps.instruction:
theory case — Command import-2 reads chunked JSON with an unknown field and a 2 MiB string, writes a report through a temporary path, crosses a time-zone boundary, and receives cancellation. Define streaming limits, atomicity, cleanup, stderr, and exit evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Reader Writer Streaming contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Reader Writer Streaming · Theory Reader Writer Streaming.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compose io.reader and io.writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer. For Predict Reader Writer Streaming · Theory Reader Writer Streaming, keep Reader Writer Streaming connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
